title: Rotational Systems
summary: This page covers mechanical system modeling using Newton's second law for translational systems, deriving the differential equation (MD2 + BD + K)y(t) = x(t) for a mass-spring-dashpot system. It then introduces rotational systems, defining torque, angular position, moment of inertia, torsional springs, and torsional dashpots with their terminal equations. The page begins Example 1.19 on deriving the input-output equation for aircraft roll angle control using ailerons.
